---
title: SQL Execution Tool
description: Run a read-only SQL query against a data product.
---

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

# SQL Execution Tool

This tool runs a SQL query against the database behind a data product and returns the result as a table. It is the workhorse behind agents like [Query](/agent-studio-docs/build/agents/query), [Data Product Query](/agent-studio-docs/build/agents/data-product-query), and [Analytics Agent](/agent-studio-docs/build/agents/analytics-agent).

Queries are read-only. Mutating statements (`UPDATE`, `DELETE`, `INSERT`, and so on) are not allowed. The data product must be backed by a SQL-compatible data source.

## When to use it

Add this tool to any agent that needs to answer questions from data by running queries. Pair it with [Get Data Schema](/agent-studio-docs/build/tools/get-data-schema-tool) so the agent can learn the available tables and columns first.

## Parameters

| Parameter | Required | Type | Suggested scope | What it does |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `data_product_id` | Yes | string | User input | The data product to query against. |
| `sql` | Yes | string | Agent determined | The query to run. |
| `result_table_name` | Yes | string | Agent determined | A short, descriptive name for the result table. |
| `data_product_version` | No | string | Fixed or User input | A specific version of the data product. |
| `pre_exec_sql` | No | string | Fixed | SQL to run before the main query, e.g. to set session context. |
| `auth_id` | No | string | Fixed or User input | The authentication credentials to use for the query. |

<Aside type="note">
`data_product_id` is a good fit for **User input** so callers choose what to query, while `sql` and `result_table_name` are normally **Agent determined** — the agent writes the query from the question. See [Configuring tool parameters](/agent-studio-docs/build/tools/configuring-tools/#configuring-tool-parameters).
</Aside>

## Example usage

```jsonc
{
  "data_product_id": "my-data-product",
  "sql": "SELECT region, SUM(amount) FROM sales GROUP BY region;",
  "result_table_name": "sales_by_region"
}
```

## Output

The query result as a table. Very large results may have rows or columns trimmed.

## See also

- [Get Data Schema Tool](/agent-studio-docs/build/tools/get-data-schema-tool)
